nativeRange.habitat: The species-level Ludwigia palustris occurs widely in marshes, ditches, slow streams, and the muddy margins of ponds and ditches across temperate and subtropical regions. The 'Super Red' cultivar is a Tropica-bred form rather than a wild population.

decorPreferences: bright lighting for deepest red coloration

summary: A compact red-stem cultivar of Ludwigia palustris bred by Tropica; widely regarded as one of the easiest red stem plants to colour up, staying smaller and redder than standard L. repens.
careNotes: Ludwigia palustris 'Super Red' is a Tropica-bred cultivar selected for compact growth and reliable red coloration. Sources broadly agree it is one of the easiest red stem plants for newer aquascapers — Tropica, Aquasabi, and Buce Plant all rate care as 'easy'. Adult submerged height varies with light and CO2: Tropica reports 10-30+ cm, Aquasabi cites 20-50 cm, Buce Plant lists 18-48 cm (7-19"), and the species-level aqua-fish.net entry cites up to 80 cm in optimal conditions — averaged here to roughly 15-50 cm for the cultivar, with most home tanks falling at the lower end. Temperature tolerance is broad: Aquasabi cites 18-28 °C and aqua-fish.net 15-25 °C; averaged to ~20-28 °C with 22-26 °C being the comfortable midpoint. pH 5.5-7.5 and soft-to-moderately-hard water (gH 2-15, kH 0-10) are both well tolerated. Lighting and CO2 are the main levers for the famous deep red color: the plant survives under low light without CO2 (and will trend gold to olive), but to push the trademark crimson coloration, sources recommend medium-to-high PAR plus CO2 supplementation. The 'nitrate-limit' method — keeping NO3 low (~5-10 ppm) while dosing iron and micronutrients heavily — is widely cited by aquascapers as the most reliable way to drive red pigmentation. Cross-source disagreement on CO2 requirement: Tropica reports 'low' CO2 demand and Aquasabi reports CO2 is not required, while Buce Plant lists CO2 as 'recommended' for best growth — averaged to 'optional' here since the plant unambiguously survives without it but colours and densifies better with it. Growth rate disagreement (Tropica 'medium' vs Buce/Aquasabi 'fast') similarly averaged to 'medium'. Placement is background or trimmed-bush midground; the compact 'Super Red' is noticeably smaller than the standard L. palustris or L. repens, making it a good choice for nano and midground placements as well as background hedges. Use nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil) for best results; root tabs help if planted in inert gravel. Trim regularly to encourage lateral branching and a denser, bushier form.
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings: cut the top of a mature stem and replant the cutting directly into the substrate, where it will quickly root. Pinching off the apical bud encourages the mother stem to produce multiple lateral side shoots, which can be trimmed off and replanted in turn for rapid mass propagation. Floating cuttings will also root in the water column before planting.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Used for cultivar identity (Tropica-bred form), height (10-30+ cm), growth rate (medium), light (low) and CO2 (low) requirements, care difficulty (easy), placement (stem plant - mid/background), and propagation (cuttings produce side shoots, cut shoots re-root quickly). The supplied search URL (tropica.com/en/search?q=Ludwigia+palustris) returned no per-species detail; resolved to the cultivar's product detail page (item 035B).

additional.name: KeepFishKeeping
additional.notes: Confirms Tropica cultivar origin, 2-4 cm wide × 10-30 cm tall per stalk, lateral side-shoot growth on trimming, intense red color with strong light and CO2, easier to colour up than L. repens 'Rubin'.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Confirms family Onagraceae, height 7-19" (~18-48 cm), care difficulty (easy), light moderate/high for color, CO2 recommended, fast growth, background placement. Notes color shifts gold-orange-red depending on conditions, and recommends aquasoil substrate.

additional.name: Aquasabi
additional.notes: Confirms family Onagraceae, height 20-50 cm, fast growth, medium light, CO2 not required, temperature 18-28 °C, pH 5-7, kH 0-14, care easy, placement middle/background or nano accent. Describes it as 'the reddest Ludwigia'.

additional.name: Aqua-Fish.net
additional.notes: Species-level reference (not cultivar-specific) used for family (Onagraceae), cosmopolitan native range (Europe/Asia/N. Africa/N. America marsh and pond margins), pH 6.5-7.5, hardness 10-20 dGH, fast growth, background placement, cuttings propagation, and iron-fertilization for color.
